name: Chile Garlic BBQ Salmon
instructions:
1. Preheat your outdoor grill to high heat. Using kitchen shears or a sharp knife, trim the tail and fins off the salmon fillet. Make several shallow diagonal cuts across the skin to help the marinade penetrate and prevent the skin from curling during cooking.

2. Lay out three large sheets of heavy-duty aluminum foil, slightly overlapping them to create a sturdy base. Place the prepared salmon fillet skin-side down in the center of the foil.

3. In a medium bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, chile sauce, minced ginger, and minced garlic until well combined. Stir in the fresh lime juice, lime zest, and brown sugar, mixing until the sugar dissolves completely.

4. Generously spoon the sauce mixture over the salmon, making sure to coat the entire surface and allowing some to pool around the fish.

5. Carefully fold the foil up and over the salmon, bringing the edges together. Crimp and seal the edges tightly to create a secure packet that will trap steam and moisture during cooking.

6. For charcoal grills, push the hot coals to one side to create an indirect heat zone. Place the foil-wrapped salmon on the cooler side of the grill (away from direct coals) and close the lid. For gas grills, place the packet on one side and turn off the burners directly beneath it, leaving the other burners on. Close the lid to maintain heat.

7. Cook for 25 to 30 minutes, until the salmon is opaque and flakes easily with a fork. Carefully transfer the packet to a serving platter, open the foil, and pour any accumulated juices over the top of the fish for extra flavor.

8. Garnish with thinly sliced green onions and serve immediately while hot.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat grill to high, trim salmon, and score the skin.

2. Whisk together soy sauce, chile sauce, ginger, garlic, lime juice, zest, and brown sugar.

3. Place salmon on foil, coat with sauce, seal tightly into a packet.

4. Grill using indirect heat for 25-30 minutes until flaky, then garnish with green onions and enjoy!
